title: Reflexive Verbs — Doing It to Yourself
description: Some German verbs point back at the subject: you wash yourself, you feel yourself well, you look forward. These need a little reflexive pronoun — and they're everywhere in daily routine.

lessons.title: Reflexive Pronouns — mich, dich, sich
lessons.subtitle: The pronoun that mirrors the subject.
lessons.hook: In English you rarely say 'I wash myself' — you just say 'I wash.' German is more explicit: 'Ich wasche mich.' That little 'mich' is a reflexive pronoun, and a whole family of everyday verbs (getting ready, feeling, relaxing) can't work without it.
lessons.explain: A reflexive verb sends the action back onto the subject, so it needs a reflexive pronoun that matches: ich → mich, du → dich, er/sie/es → sich, wir → uns, ihr → euch, sie/Sie → sich. Example: sich waschen → ich wasche mich, du wäschst dich, er wäscht sich. Notice 'sich' covers he/she/it and they/you-formal. Most of these use the Accusative reflexive pronoun (shown here). Reflexive verbs are extremely common for daily routine and emotions, so learning the pronoun set pays off immediately.

vocabulary.memoryHook: Reflexive + stem-changer: du wäschst dich, er wäscht sich.

vocabulary.exampleSentence: Ich freue mich auf das Wochenende.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I'm looking forward to the weekend.
vocabulary.memoryHook: sich freuen auf + Akk = look forward to. A very common emotion verb.
With 'uns/euch' it means 'each other'. Reflexive + reciprocal.
Always reflexive: 'Ich fühle mich …' + adjective (gut, müde, krank).
'Setz dich!' — a friendly invitation to sit.

exercises.explanation: The reflexive pronoun for 'ich' is 'mich': 'Ich fühle mich gut.'

exercises.prompt: Which reflexive pronoun goes with 'er/sie/es' AND 'sie/Sie'?
exercises.explanation: 'sich' is used for er/sie/es and for sie/Sie: 'Er freut sich', 'Sie treffen sich'. It's the pronoun the verbs are named after (sich freuen).

exercises.prompt: Complete: 'Wir treffen ___ um acht.' (We meet at eight.)
exercises.explanation: The reflexive/reciprocal pronoun for 'wir' is 'uns': 'Wir treffen uns um acht' (we meet each other).

lessons.title: Daily Routine with Reflexive Verbs
lessons.subtitle: Describe your morning: get up, shower, get dressed, hurry.
lessons.hook: Reflexive verbs are the language of routine. 'I get up, I shower, I get dressed, I hurry.' If you can string these together, you can describe an entire day in German — one of the first things a real conversation asks for.
lessons.explain: Many daily-routine verbs are reflexive: sich duschen (to shower), sich anziehen (to get dressed), sich beeilen (to hurry), sich ausruhen (to rest). Some are also separable — sich anziehen splits AND is reflexive: 'Ich ziehe mich an.' In the Perfect tense they take haben and keep the reflexive pronoun: 'Ich habe mich geduscht.' Order tip: the reflexive pronoun usually comes right after the conjugated verb: 'Ich dusche mich', 'Morgens dusche ich mich.'

examples.german: Ich dusche mich und ziehe mich an.
Beeil dich, wir sind spät dran!
Am Abend ruhe ich mich aus.

vocabulary.memoryHook: Reflexive: 'Ich dusche mich.' Perfect: 'Ich habe mich geduscht.'
Reflexive AND separable: 'Ich ziehe mich an.' Opposite: sich ausziehen.
'Beeil dich!' = Hurry up! A phrase you'll hear (and need) often.

vocabulary.exampleSentence: Im Winter ziehe ich mich warm an.
vocabulary.memoryHook: Pair with clothes vocabulary for a full routine description.

exercises.prompt: Complete: 'Ich ___ mich jeden Morgen.' (I shower every morning.)
exercises.explanation: sich duschen → 'Ich dusche mich'. The verb takes the normal -e ending for 'ich', and 'mich' is the reflexive pronoun.

exercises.prompt: 'sich anziehen' is special because it is…
exercises.explanation: sich anziehen is reflexive (needs mich/dich/sich) and separable (prefix 'an' goes to the end): 'Ich ziehe mich an.'

exercises.prompt: Complete the Perfect: 'Ich habe mich ___.' (I showered.) — verb: sich duschen
exercises.explanation: sich duschen → geduscht, with 'haben' and the reflexive pronoun kept: 'Ich habe mich geduscht.'
